H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (Over-stimulation L1, n=95, mean 7.15, std 1.78, confidence=strong) is the highest-n protocol with broad right-weighted distribution (scores 7–10 = 66%). Qualitative (n=29) confirms 'Noticeable improvement' plurality with 10-minute settle and balanced rhythm. YouTube reach (30 views) is triple the next highest in HEALING, despite no retention data. AutoGluon predicts 6.86 for overstim zone — below observed, suggesting conservative model estimate. This is a cornerstone protocol with strong efficacy and best discoverability.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: This is the most popular protocol so far — 30 views — with most people feeling strong, lasting calm and noticing improvement. Those who found it loved it.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (Over-stimulation L2, n=51, mean 6.92, std 1.73, confidence=strong) reaches strong confidence with distribution peaked at score 7 (14) dropping sharply above 9. Qualitative settle is slow (10-minute mode) with music opinion split between 'Liked it' (10) and 'Neutral' (6), which may suppress upper tail. YouTube minimal (5 views). Outcome closely matches AutoGluon prediction (6.86), indicating reasonable calibration.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners felt gradual improvement over about 10 minutes, with mixed feelings about the music. Very few people have seen it online.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (Over-stimulation L3, n=47, mean 7.04, std 1.68, confidence=meaningful) recovers above 7 with balanced distribution (scores 6–9 account for 37 of 47). Qualitative shows unexpected 'Trying to sleep' activity signal (8 of 23) — a cross-entry routing opportunity the model hasn't captured. Music opinion solid (11 'Liked it'), settle fast (5-minute mode). Predicted score aligns with observation.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: People felt calmer, often using it to help sleep, with balanced and settling rhythms. Reach is still very low.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (Anxiety & Stress L1, n=67, mean 7.22, std 1.93, confidence=strong) leads the dataset with 63% positive ratings. Distribution shows mode at score 9 with right tail extending to 10 (7 responses). Qualitative data (n=23) shows 'Moderate Improvement' plurality with 10-minute settle, balanced rhythm, and strong music approval (16 'Liked' or 'Loved'). YouTube reach is minimal (7 views, no retention data). AutoGluon predicts 7.838 for anxiety zone — 0.62 above observed, suggesting modest design tuning could close the gap. The protocol is well-calibrated but under-discovered.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners felt a noticeable to moderate lift in anxiety after about 10 minutes, with rhythms they found balanced and calming. Only a few people have discovered the video so far.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (Anxiety & Stress L2, n=48, mean 6.94, std 1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows tighter variance than L1 with distribution peaked at score 7 (15 responses). Qualitative (n=20) shows balanced listener mix (12 self, 6 clinicians) with fast 5-minute settle. Music opinion leans neutral ('Neutral' 5, 'Liked it' 9), which may cap ceiling. AutoGluon does not route directly here; the 6.94 mean is stable and approaches meaningful signal strength.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: People reported feeling calmer within about 5 minutes, with the music just ok for some. The protocol works but hasn't found its audience online yet.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (Anxiety & Stress L3, n=36, mean 7.08, std 1.89, confidence=meaningful) shows secondary mass at high scores (9–10 each with 5 responses) alongside primary peak at 6–7, suggesting possible responder subgroup. Qualitative settle_time is bimodal (10 min vs end-of-session), indicating the longer arc divides listeners into mid-session and full-arc responders. YouTube views minimal (4). Predicted anxiety zone (7.84) exceeds observed, flagging potential for parameter adjustment.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners noticed a calming shift around ten minutes, with some needing the full session to settle. The video reach is still very low.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (Crisis L1, n=43, mean 6.35, std 1.70, confidence=meaningful) is the lowest HEALING mean — structurally expected for acute distress entry. Distribution peaks at score 8 (12 responses) with no scores at 10. Qualitative data (n=24) shows striking tension: 'Significant improvement' (8) and 'Noticeable improvement' (7) dominate change ratings, with 16 of 24 reporting positive music opinion, yet 0–10 scale shows only 2 responses at 10 — a potential scale compression effect in crisis contexts. AutoGluon selects H-CR-L2 for crisis zone (7.96 predicted).
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Some people in crisis felt a significant improvement, reporting fast relief, but the overall scores are moderate. The video is hardly being seen.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (Crisis L2, n=33, mean 6.52, std 1.70, confidence=meaningful) performs slightly above L1 with distribution centered at 7–8. Qualitative (n=16) shows mixed ratings: 'Moderate Improvement' leads (6), but music opinion splits 5 'Loved it' vs 6 'Neutral' — the groove divides listeners. AutoGluon selects H-CR-L2 as top crisis protocol with predicted 7.96 — the largest prediction gap (1.44) in HEALING, indicating design parameters underperform potential.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: People felt moderate improvement, but the music divided opinions — some loved it, others were neutral. Few have found it online.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (Crisis L3, n=27, mean 6.52, std 1.91, confidence=meaningful) shares mean with L2 but higher variance and more right-skew (3 responses at 9–10). Qualitative settle_time is notably fast ('Immediately' 7 of 19), suggesting the longer arc entrains quickly for some. Music opinion shows 4 'Not liked' responses — highest explicit dislike in crisis family. AutoGluon over-predicts, indicating the groove may polarize despite decent outcomes.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: A few listeners felt immediate relief, but overall scores land in the middle range with some explicit dislikes. Not widely discovered yet.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (Sleep L1, n=54, mean 7.19, std 1.86, confidence=strong) leads sleep family with broad right distribution (scores 8–10 = 25 of 54). Qualitative (n=15) shows strong self-listener majority (13 of 15) with fast settle (5-minute mode, 3 'Immediately'), consistent with independent use and quick response. AutoGluon selects H-SL-L1 for sleep zone (predicted 7.539), closely matching observed 7.19.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Most people felt strong improvement quickly, settling within 5 minutes. Self-listeners love it, but reach is minimal.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (Sleep L2, n=45, mean 7.09, std 1.55, confidence=meaningful) shows tightest std in HEALING with compact distribution at 7–8. Qualitative (n=15) reveals unusual listener inversion: clinicians (6) and caregivers (5) outnumber self-listeners (4), and 3 respondents found rhythm 'Too busy' — anomalous for sleep protocol. May need groove adjustment. No AutoGluon routing here.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Clinicians and caregivers use this more than individuals, with some finding the rhythm too busy for sleep. Scores are solid but mixed.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (Sleep L3, n=28, mean 7.07, std 1.68, confidence=meaningful) is stable but under-sampled; distribution central-peaked at 7–8 with no scores below 3. Qualitative (n=22) shows good listener diversity (12 self, 7 caregiver, 3 clinician) with 5-minute settle mode, but 7 rated rhythm as 'Slightly busy' — highest in sleep family, potentially suppressing upper tail.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: People felt good improvement and settled relatively quickly, though some felt the rhythm was slightly busy. Low reach.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (Focus & Clarity L1, n=41, mean 6.46, std 1.82, confidence=meaningful) is lowest THRIVING mean despite high n. Distribution peaks at 7 (12) but has heavy lower tail (8 responses at 3–5). AutoGluon routes cross-bucket here from anxiety, focus, and motivation zones but observed undershoots by ~1.3–1.5 points — largest delta in THRIVING. Qualitative shows fast settle (6 'Immediately', 7 at 5 min), 'Slightly busy' rhythm opinion (9 of 23), likely suppressing scores. Protocol entrains quickly but doesn't sustain.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: People settled quickly but the effects didn't last, with some finding the rhythm slightly busy. Scores are lower than expected.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (Focus & Clarity L2, n=32, mean 7.19, std 1.60, confidence=meaningful) recovers strongly from L1 — distribution peaks at 8 (10) with clean right skew, only 1 response at 3. Qualitative (n=17) shows high caregiver/clinician mix (7 of 17) and 10-minute settle dominance (9), suggesting slower but deeper entrainment. Music opinion split ('Neutral' 6, 'Loved it' 5). Approaches AutoGluon prediction.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: People felt moderate to significant improvement with deeper entrainment around 10 minutes. Solid performance starting to be discovered.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (Focus & Clarity L3, n=35, mean 7.63, std 1.97, confidence=meaningful) is THRIVING's top performer with 71% positive ratings and mode at 10 (10 responses) despite gap at 9 (only 1). Distribution hints at bimodal responder split. Qualitative (n=29) shows 11 'Significant improvement', 15 'Balanced' rhythm, and 10-minute settle mode (15). YouTube reach (15) highest in THRIVING. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, closely matching observation — confirmed strong performance.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: This is the standout protocol — most people felt significant improvement, especially those who listened longer. Strong discovery signal (15 views).

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (Creative Flow L1, n=34, mean 6.74, std 1.73, confidence=meaningful) shows truncated upper tail (only 6 responses at 9–10 combined). Qualitative (n=21) unusual: settle_time splits evenly between 'Immediately' (6) and 'About 10 minutes' (6), suggesting two entrainment populations. Change_rating shows 'Slight improvement' dominant (8) — weakest plurality in dataset. YouTube negligible (2 views). May need groove adjustment.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Some people settled immediately, others needed 10 minutes — it seems to work differently for different listeners. Overall feeling is slight improvement.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (Creative Flow L2, n=24, mean 6.79, std 1.41, confidence=meaningful) has narrow std with distribution peaked at 6–8. Qualitative (n=15) is emotionally positive: 'Loved it' (6) and 'Significant improvement' (5) lead, with universally positive music opinion — no 'Neutral' or 'Disliked'. Gap between strong emotional valence and mid-range scores may indicate 0–10 scale compression for Creative Flow.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: People loved the music and felt significant improvement, though the scores are moderate. Almost no one has seen it online yet.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (Creative Flow L3, n=18, mean 7.28, std 1.32, confidence=developing) posts highest Creative Flow mean despite small n, with tight distribution at 6–8. AutoGluon semantically clusters this with Maintenance & Balance (sim 0.771–0.798) rather than Creative Flow siblings — a significant parameter drift flag. Qualitative settle splits 5/10 minutes (7 each), rhythm 'Balanced' (14 of 25). Requires Hz/movement audit.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners noticed strong improvement and found the rhythm balanced — it works well but hasn't been widely discovered.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (Maintenance & Balance L1, n=25, mean 7.04, std 1.34, confidence=meaningful) has tight std with distribution at 6–9. Qualitative (n=23) shows moderate improvement and 20-minute settle majority (9), the slowest in THRIVING — longer entrainment curve. Music opinion neutral-leaning ('Neutral/Liked' 6 each). AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance zone, suggesting hold extension could improve outcomes.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: People felt moderate improvement but needed about 20 minutes to settle — the longest entrainment in the series. Good potential, low reach.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (Maintenance & Balance L2, n=22, mean 6.68, std 2.03, confidence=meaningful) shows highest variance in maintenance family with sparse distribution. Qualitative (n=16) shows moderate improvement and 5-minute settle (6), with balanced/slightly busy rhythm split (7 each). AutoGluon predicts 7.69 — substantial gap. Highest drift in embedding (0.637).
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: People felt noticeable improvement with quick settle times, though some found the rhythm a bit busy. Needs more data.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (Maintenance & Balance L3, n=14, mean 6.93, std 1.73, confidence=developing) shows 71% positive despite low n. Distribution centered at 7–8 with quick settle (8 at 5 min). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, indicating untapped potential. Embedding drift lowest in dataset (0.557), meaning analytical framing has substantively shifted as data evolved.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Despite low numbers, most people felt positive outcomes and settled quickly. Needs more data to confirm signal.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (Mood & Energy L1, n=39, mean 6.74, std 1.94, confidence=meaningful) shows 54% positive with distribution spread across 4–10. Qualitative shows moderate/significant improvement (7 each), mixed settle times, and strong music love (10 'Loved it'). YouTube (8 views). AutoGluon predicts highest low-affect score (8.23) — gap of 1.49. High emotional valence with modest outcome suggests parameter adjustment opportunity.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: People loved the music and felt significant improvement, but the scores don't fully reflect that emotional response. Strong potential.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (Mood & Energy L2, n=25, mean 6.76, std 1.71, confidence=meaningful) shows 64% positive — highest in mood/energy. Qualitative shows 'Significant improvement' (7) dominates, with 20-minute settle (4). Music strongly positive (7 'Liked', 6 'Loved'). YouTube (8 views). AutoGluon predicts 8.23 — gap of 1.47. Cross-bucket routing to this protocol suggests design potential.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: People felt significant improvement with strong music appeal, though settle times are longer. Good potential.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (Mood & Energy L3, n=18, mean 6.28, std 1.74, confidence=developing) shows 56% positive with distribution centered 5–8. Qualitative shows 'Noticeable' (9) with 5-minute settle (8). Music positive (11 'Liked', 7 'Loved'). Lowest among mood/energy siblings despite developing n. AutoGluon over-predicts, suggesting design may not sustain entrainment.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: People noticed improvement with quick settle, but scores are lower than siblings. May need entrainment adjustment.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (Motivation & Drive L1, n=22, mean 6.36, std 2.01, confidence=meaningful) is lowest TRANSFORMING mean with 45% positive — lowest positivity in series. Heavy lower tail (4 at score 4). Qualitative shows 'Noticeable' (6), 'Significant' (5), with 5-minute settle (9). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — largest delta (1.52) in entire dataset. This is a major underperformer requiring ascent curve recalibration.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Despite AutoGluon predicting strong performance, actual scores are low with high variance. The ascent curve may need recalibration.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (Motivation & Drive L2, n=14, mean 6.21, std 1.63, confidence=developing) shows 43% positive with sparse distribution, only 1 response at 9, none at 10. Qualitative (n=21) shows 'Noticeable/Moderate' (6 each), slow settle (20 min and 10 min tied at 5). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — gap of 1.67 (largest alongside L1). Requires intensity adjustment.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: People felt only slight to moderate improvement, with slow settle times. The protocol intensity may be too low.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (Motivation & Drive L3, n=13, mean 7.15, std 2.38, confidence=developing) shows highest TRANSFORMING mean but also highest variance (2.38) — distribution [0,0,0,1,2,0,2,1,2,3,2] is sparse and erratic. Qualitative shows moderate improvement (8) and quick settle (5 min). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — gap of 0.73. High variance and sparse data suggest population mismatch; two analyzers flag as variant candidate.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: High variance with some strong positive outcomes — might work well for certain subgroups but not consistently. Needs a variant.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (Mind & Clarity L1, n=24, mean 6.75, std 1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows 54% positive with 2 adverse reports ('made things worse') — only protocol with explicit negative outcomes. Distribution peaks at 6–7. Qualitative shows balanced rhythm, 'Loved it' (9) equalling 'Liked it' (9). YouTube (8 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for X-MC-L2, slightly above observed — underperformance flagged.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Most people felt noticeable improvement, but two people said it actually made things worse — the only adverse reports. Needs investigation.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (Mind & Clarity L2, n=16, mean 6.81, std 1.64, confidence=developing) shows 56% positive with distribution at 6–8. Qualitative (n=20) shows 'Significant improvement' (7) and 'Very settling' rhythm (12) dominating. YouTube (12 views) highest in mind/clarity. AutoGluon selects this for zone prediction (7.54), indicating design parameters are sound but n limits confidence.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: People felt significant improvement with very settling rhythms — the best in the mind/clarity category. Moderate discovery (12 views).

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (Mind & Clarity L3, n=10, mean 5.70, std 2.45, confidence=developing) is the lowest-scoring protocol across all series with 50% positive — no better than chance. Distribution sparse with 1 at 2, 1 at 3, 2 at 4 — a heavy lower tail. Qualitative (n=17) shows mixed outcomes ('Noticeable' 9, 'Moderate' 3). Highest variance (2.45) and lowest mean flag this as critical redesign candidate. Embedding shows drift (0.778).
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: This is the lowest-scoring protocol — people felt mixed results with high variance. May need redesign.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset (n=1000) shows HEALING and THRIVING series performing comparably (~6.97 mean) with tight variance, while TRANSFORMING trails at 6.59 with expectedly higher dispersion reflecting ascending-arc design for low-affect states.
• HEALING (n=574) protocols dominate upper score range, with H-AX-L1 (7.22) and H-SL-L1 (7.19) leading; crisis protocols (H-CR-*) form the series' low cluster as structurally expected.
• THRIVING (n=245) shows Focus & Clarity outperforming Creative Flow, with T-FC-L3 (7.63, 71% positive) the clear outlier.
• TRANSFORMING (n=181) carries wider spread and lower means, with X-MC-L3 (5.7) the lowest-scoring protocol across all series.
• YouTube reach is uniformly low (≤30 views) yet positive outcomes exceed 60% for many protocols — a substantial discovery gap.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series display efficacy patterns consistent with their therapeutic design intent.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols achieve the strongest and most consistent outcomes, particularly for anxiety (7.22 mean) and sleep (7.19 mean) entry points, with lowest variance across subcategories.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols show a clear level-gradient in Focus & Clarity — longer protocols yield higher scores (T-FC-L3 at 7.63 vs T-FC-L1 at 6.46) — supporting the hold-phase entrainment hypothesis.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols exhibit the widest spread and lowest means (5.7–7.15), with Motivation & Drive particularly underperforming (X-MD-L1 at 6.36), suggesting ascent curves may need recalibration for listener readiness.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several distributional and model-prediction mismatches warrant investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (mean 5.7, std 2.45, n=10) is the lowest-scoring protocol across all series and the only one with 50% positive rating — a high-priority redesign candidate.
• T-FC-L3 shows bimodal distribution with secondary peak at score 10 despite only 1 response at 9, suggesting a possible responder/non-responder split.
• AutoGluon prediction-observed deltas are substantial: H-CR-L2 (predicted 7.96 vs observed 6.52, delta 1.44) and X-MD-L1 (predicted 7.88 vs observed 6.36, delta 1.52) indicate design-parameter underperformance.
• X-MC-L1 is the only protocol with explicit adverse reports (2 'It actually made things worse' responses at n=24).
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Four actionable signals emerge from convergent analyzer observations.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L1 (n=41) has low observed mean (6.46) despite AutoGluon cross-bucket routing from anxiety, focus, and motivation — three analyzers flag the hold-phase may be too brief; ACTION: extend hold duration and A/B test.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MD-L3 (n=13) shows highest variance (std 2.38) with sparse erratic distribution — two analyzers suggest population mismatch; ACTION: create smoother ascent variant with reduced delta and test against original.
• H-OS-L1 (n=95) has strong confidence, highest YouTube reach (30 views), and observed mean (7.15) exceeds AutoGluon prediction (6.86) — this protocol is well-tuned and a candidate for amplification.
• T-CF-L3 (n=18, developing) is semantically clustered with Maintenance & Balance protocols by AutoGluon rather than its Creative Flow siblings — ACTION: audit Hz/movement parameters for functional zone drift.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Cross-run drift analysis shows notable analytical instability in Maintenance & Balance and Creative Flow subcategories.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: T-MB-L3 (0.557), T-MB-L1 (0.565), T-CF-L3 (0.586), and T-MB-L2 (0.637) show similarity below 0.65 between earliest and most recent runs — this indicates genuine data evolution and expanded n has substantively shifted Sphere's interpretive framing.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-MB-L2 ↔ T-MB-L3 (0.844) and T-MB-L1 ↔ T-MB-L3 (0.823) form a tight semantic subgroup, with T-CF-L3 pulled into this cluster — indicating Maintenance & Balance protocols share analytical features distinct from their THRIVING series siblings.
• T-CF-L3 shows the lowest mean pairwise similarity (0.742), making it the most analytically distinct protocol in the dataset.

by_protocol.T-CF-L1: Ember (Creative Flow L1, n=34, mean 6.74, std 1.73, confidence=meaningful) shows truncated upper tail (only 6 responses at 9–10 combined). Qualitative (n=21) unusual: settle_time splits evenly between 'Immediately' (6) and 'About 10 minutes' (6), suggesting two entrainment populations. Change_rating shows 'Slight improvement' dominant (8) — weakest plurality in dataset. YouTube negligible (2 views). May need groove adjustment.
by_protocol.T-CF-L2: Current (Creative Flow L2, n=24, mean 6.79, std 1.41, confidence=meaningful) has narrow std with distribution peaked at 6–8. Qualitative (n=15) is emotionally positive: 'Loved it' (6) and 'Significant improvement' (5) lead, with universally positive music opinion — no 'Neutral' or 'Disliked'. Gap between strong emotional valence and mid-range scores may indicate 0–10 scale compression for Creative Flow.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3: Aurora (Creative Flow L3, n=18, mean 7.28, std 1.32, confidence=developing) posts highest Creative Flow mean despite small n, with tight distribution at 6–8. AutoGluon semantically clusters this with Maintenance & Balance (sim 0.771–0.798) rather than Creative Flow siblings — a significant parameter drift flag. Qualitative settle splits 5/10 minutes (7 each), rhythm 'Balanced' (14 of 25). Requires Hz/movement audit.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: Compass (Focus & Clarity L1, n=41, mean 6.46, std 1.82, confidence=meaningful) is lowest THRIVING mean despite high n. Distribution peaks at 7 (12) but has heavy lower tail (8 responses at 3–5). AutoGluon routes cross-bucket here from anxiety, focus, and motivation zones but observed undershoots by ~1.3–1.5 points — largest delta in THRIVING. Qualitative shows fast settle (6 'Immediately', 7 at 5 min), 'Slightly busy' rhythm opinion (9 of 23), likely suppressing scores. Protocol entrains quickly but doesn't sustain.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: Waypoint (Focus & Clarity L2, n=32, mean 7.19, std 1.60, confidence=meaningful) recovers strongly from L1 — distribution peaks at 8 (10) with clean right skew, only 1 response at 3. Qualitative (n=17) shows high caregiver/clinician mix (7 of 17) and 10-minute settle dominance (9), suggesting slower but deeper entrainment. Music opinion split ('Neutral' 6, 'Loved it' 5). Approaches AutoGluon prediction.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: Summit (Focus & Clarity L3, n=35, mean 7.63, std 1.97, confidence=meaningful) is THRIVING's top performer with 71% positive ratings and mode at 10 (10 responses) despite gap at 9 (only 1). Distribution hints at bimodal responder split. Qualitative (n=29) shows 11 'Significant improvement', 15 'Balanced' rhythm, and 10-minute settle mode (15). YouTube reach (15) highest in THRIVING. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, closely matching observation — confirmed strong performance.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: Stone (Maintenance & Balance L1, n=25, mean 7.04, std 1.34, confidence=meaningful) has tight std with distribution at 6–9. Qualitative (n=23) shows moderate improvement and 20-minute settle majority (9), the slowest in THRIVING — longer entrainment curve. Music opinion neutral-leaning ('Neutral/Liked' 6 each). AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance zone, suggesting hold extension could improve outcomes.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: Axis (Maintenance & Balance L2, n=22, mean 6.68, std 2.03, confidence=meaningful) shows highest variance in maintenance family with sparse distribution. Qualitative (n=16) shows moderate improvement and 5-minute settle (6), with balanced/slightly busy rhythm split (7 each). AutoGluon predicts 7.69 — substantial gap. Highest drift in embedding (0.637).
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: Orbit (Maintenance & Balance L3, n=14, mean 6.93, std 1.73, confidence=developing) shows 71% positive despite low n. Distribution centered at 7–8 with quick settle (8 at 5 min). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, indicating untapped potential. Embedding drift lowest in dataset (0.557), meaning analytical framing has substantively shifted as data evolved.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: Cavern (Mind & Clarity L1, n=24, mean 6.75, std 1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows 54% positive with 2 adverse reports ('made things worse') — only protocol with explicit negative outcomes. Distribution peaks at 6–7. Qualitative shows balanced rhythm, 'Loved it' (9) equalling 'Liked it' (9). YouTube (8 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for X-MC-L2, slightly above observed — underperformance flagged.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: Starlight (Mind & Clarity L2, n=16, mean 6.81, std 1.64, confidence=developing) shows 56% positive with distribution at 6–8. Qualitative (n=20) shows 'Significant improvement' (7) and 'Very settling' rhythm (12) dominating. YouTube (12 views) highest in mind/clarity. AutoGluon selects this for zone prediction (7.54), indicating design parameters are sound but n limits confidence.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: Cosmos (Mind & Clarity L3, n=10, mean 5.70, std 2.45, confidence=developing) is the lowest-scoring protocol across all series with 50% positive — no better than chance. Distribution sparse with 1 at 2, 1 at 3, 2 at 4 — a heavy lower tail. Qualitative (n=17) shows mixed outcomes ('Noticeable' 9, 'Moderate' 3). Highest variance (2.45) and lowest mean flag this as critical redesign candidate. Embedding shows drift (0.778).
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: Footpath (Motivation & Drive L1, n=22, mean 6.36, std 2.01, confidence=meaningful) is lowest TRANSFORMING mean with 45% positive — lowest positivity in series. Heavy lower tail (4 at score 4). Qualitative shows 'Noticeable' (6), 'Significant' (5), with 5-minute settle (9). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — largest delta (1.52) in entire dataset. This is a major underperformer requiring ascent curve recalibration.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: Trail (Motivation & Drive L2, n=14, mean 6.21, std 1.63, confidence=developing) shows 43% positive with sparse distribution, only 1 response at 9, none at 10. Qualitative (n=21) shows 'Noticeable/Moderate' (6 each), slow settle (20 min and 10 min tied at 5). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — gap of 1.67 (largest alongside L1). Requires intensity adjustment.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: Switchback (Motivation & Drive L3, n=13, mean 7.15, std 2.38, confidence=developing) shows highest TRANSFORMING mean but also highest variance (2.38) — distribution [0,0,0,1,2,0,2,1,2,3,2] is sparse and erratic. Qualitative shows moderate improvement (8) and quick settle (5 min). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — gap of 0.73. High variance and sparse data suggest population mismatch; two analyzers flag as variant candidate.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: Breeze (Mood & Energy L1, n=39, mean 6.74, std 1.94, confidence=meaningful) shows 54% positive with distribution spread across 4–10. Qualitative shows moderate/significant improvement (7 each), mixed settle times, and strong music love (10 'Loved it'). YouTube (8 views). AutoGluon predicts highest low-affect score (8.23) — gap of 1.49. High emotional valence with modest outcome suggests parameter adjustment opportunity.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: Sunrise (Mood & Energy L2, n=25, mean 6.76, std 1.71, confidence=meaningful) shows 64% positive — highest in mood/energy. Qualitative shows 'Significant improvement' (7) dominates, with 20-minute settle (4). Music strongly positive (7 'Liked', 6 'Loved'). YouTube (8 views). AutoGluon predicts 8.23 — gap of 1.47. Cross-bucket routing to this protocol suggests design potential.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: Updraft (Mood & Energy L3, n=18, mean 6.28, std 1.74, confidence=developing) shows 56% positive with distribution centered 5–8. Qualitative shows 'Noticeable' (9) with 5-minute settle (8). Music positive (11 'Liked', 7 'Loved'). Lowest among mood/energy siblings despite developing n. AutoGluon over-predicts, suggesting design may not sustain entrainment.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners felt a noticeable to moderate lift in anxiety after about 10 minutes, with rhythms they found balanced and calming. Only a few people have discovered the video so far.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: People reported feeling calmer within about 5 minutes, with the music just ok for some. The protocol works but hasn't found its audience online yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Listeners noticed a calming shift around ten minutes, with some needing the full session to settle. The video reach is still very low.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Some people in crisis felt a significant improvement, reporting fast relief, but the overall scores are moderate. The video is hardly being seen.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: People felt moderate improvement, but the music divided opinions — some loved it, others were neutral. Few have found it online.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: A few listeners felt immediate relief, but overall scores land in the middle range with some explicit dislikes. Not widely discovered yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: This is the most popular protocol so far — 30 views — with most people feeling strong, lasting calm and noticing improvement. Those who found it loved it.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Listeners felt gradual improvement over about 10 minutes, with mixed feelings about the music. Very few people have seen it online.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: People felt calmer, often using it to help sleep, with balanced and settling rhythms. Reach is still very low.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Most people felt strong improvement quickly, settling within 5 minutes. Self-listeners love it, but reach is minimal.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Clinicians and caregivers use this more than individuals, with some finding the rhythm too busy for sleep. Scores are solid but mixed.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: People felt good improvement and settled relatively quickly, though some felt the rhythm was slightly busy. Low reach.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Some people settled immediately, others needed 10 minutes — it seems to work differently for different listeners. Overall feeling is slight improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: People loved the music and felt significant improvement, though the scores are moderate. Almost no one has seen it online yet.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Listeners noticed strong improvement and found the rhythm balanced — it works well but hasn't been widely discovered.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: People settled quickly but the effects didn't last, with some finding the rhythm slightly busy. Scores are lower than expected.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: People felt moderate to significant improvement with deeper entrainment around 10 minutes. Solid performance starting to be discovered.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: This is the standout protocol — most people felt significant improvement, especially those who listened longer. Strong discovery signal (15 views).
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: People felt moderate improvement but needed about 20 minutes to settle — the longest entrainment in the series. Good potential, low reach.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: People felt noticeable improvement with quick settle times, though some found the rhythm a bit busy. Needs more data.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Despite low numbers, most people felt positive outcomes and settled quickly. Needs more data to confirm signal.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Most people felt noticeable improvement, but two people said it actually made things worse — the only adverse reports. Needs investigation.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: People felt significant improvement with very settling rhythms — the best in the mind/clarity category. Moderate discovery (12 views).
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: This is the lowest-scoring protocol — people felt mixed results with high variance. May need redesign.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Despite AutoGluon predicting strong performance, actual scores are low with high variance. The ascent curve may need recalibration.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: People felt only slight to moderate improvement, with slow settle times. The protocol intensity may be too low.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: High variance with some strong positive outcomes — might work well for certain subgroups but not consistently. Needs a variant.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: People loved the music and felt significant improvement, but the scores don't fully reflect that emotional response. Strong potential.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: People felt significant improvement with strong music appeal, though settle times are longer. Good potential.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: People noticed improvement with quick settle, but scores are lower than siblings. May need entrainment adjustment.
